name: correct
description: Track corrections and undo mutations. action: record — log correction. action: list — pending corrections. action: resolve — mark resolved. action: undo — reverse last mutation. Returns correction record, list, or undo result. Does not modify note content directly. e.g. { action:"record", path:"people/alice.md", entity:"Alice", note:"Wrong title" } { action:"undo" }

path.description: [record|list] Note path the correction applies to

correction_id.description: [resolve] ID of the correction to mark resolved

name: discover_tools
description: Find and activate specialized tools. Call with what you need — e.g. "vault health", "graph connections", "schema migration". Returns matching tool names, descriptions, and input schemas. Does not execute discovered tools — call them separately after discovery.

query.description: Natural language description of what you need — e.g. "vault health", "backlinks and graph", "schema migration"

name: doctor
title: Doctor
description: Use for diagnostics and runtime management. Returns vault health, named checks, stats, pipeline status, server log, or config. Does not read note content — use read or search for that. action: health | diagnosis | stats | pipeline | config | log

action.description: Operation: health=vault status, pipeline=indexing status, config=runtime config, log=activity log

detail.description: [health] Detail level: summary or full. [pipeline] true = include per-step timings

key.description: [config] Config key to update (required for set)

value.description: [config] New value for the key (required for set)

since.description: [log] Only return entries after this Unix timestamp (ms)

component.description: [log] Filter by component (server, index, fts5, semantic, tasks, watcher, statedb, config)

name: edit_section
description: Edit content inside a named section of a vault note. action: add — append/prepend under the heading (auto-wikilinks). action: remove — delete matching text. action: replace — find-and-replace within the section. Returns mutation result with path, section, preview. Does not touch other sections.

action.description: Operation to perform: add | remove | replace

section.description: Heading text to edit (e.g., "Log" or "## Log")

content.description: [add] Content to insert under the section

create_if_missing.description: [add] If true and the note does not exist, create it from template first

format.description: [add] How to format the content (default: plain)

suggestOutgoingLinks.description: [add|replace] Append suggested outgoing wikilinks based on content

maxSuggestions.description: [add|replace] Maximum number of suggested wikilinks (1-10, default: 5)

content.description: Pre-neutralized text; sanitized and indented by this tool

children.description: [add] Labeled sub-bullets appended under the parent content line

linkedEntities.description: [add] Entity names already linked in the content. When skipWikilinks=true, these are tracked for feedback without re-processing.

shard.description: [add] Route append-only operational content into bounded shard notes and link them from the requested note

pattern.description: [remove] Text or pattern to match for removal

search.description: [replace] Text or pattern to search for

mode.description: [remove|replace] Which matches to act on (default: first)

commit.description: If true, commit this change to git (creates undo point)

dry_run.description: Preview changes without writing to disk

name: entity
description: Manage vault entities and aliases. action: list — browse by category. alias — add an alternate name. suggest_aliases — find missing aliases. merge — absorb one entity into another and rewire links. suggest_merges — find duplicates. dismiss_merge — mark a merge suggestion incorrect. dismiss_prospect — reject an unresolved prospect term so it stops surfacing as an active stub candidate. Returns lists, results, or candidates. Does not create notes.

include.description: [list] Extra per-entity aggregates to attach: backlink_count (distinct notes linking to the entity name or any of its aliases) and recency ({ lastMentionedAt, mentionCount } or null). Omitted → lean response. aliases + isSuppressed are always present regardless.

entity.description: [alias] Entity path to add alias to; [suggest_aliases] entity to get suggestions for

source_name.description: [alias] Compatibility form: entity name to absorb as an alias of target_path, rewriting links. [dismiss_merge] Source entity name

target_path.description: [alias] Compatibility form: target note path for source_name absorption. [dismiss_merge] Target entity path

dry_run.description: [alias|merge] Preview the operation without writing: alias absorption or entity merge plan

secondary.description: [merge] Entity path to absorb into primary

prospect.description: [dismiss_prospect] Prospect term or display name to reject

note_path.description: [dismiss_prospect] Optional note path that motivated the dismissal

name: find_notes
description: Enumerate notes by metadata — folder, tags, or frontmatter values. Use when you need a structural list, not relevance-ranked search. Returns lightweight note summaries (path, title, modified, frontmatter, tags). Does not perform full-text or semantic search — for concept search, use search instead.

folder.description: Filter to notes inside this folder (and its subfolders)

where.description: Frontmatter filters as key-value pairs. Example: { "type": "project", "status": "active" }

has_tag.description: Filter to notes with this tag

has_any_tag.description: Filter to notes with any of these tags

has_all_tags.description: Filter to notes with all of these tags

include_children.description: When true, tag filters also match child tags (e.g., has_tag: "project" also matches "project/active")

title_contains.description: Filter to notes whose title contains this text (case-insensitive)

modified_after.description: Only notes modified after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

modified_before.description: Only notes modified before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

detail_count.description: Number of top results with full metadata. Remaining get lightweight summaries. Default: 5.

name: graph
title: Graph
description: Vault graph analysis. action: analyse — hubs/orphans/clusters. action: backlinks/forward_links — links to/from note. action: strong_connections — top links. action: path — shortest chain. action: neighbors — shared connections. action: strength — link weight. action: cooccurrence_gaps — unlinked co-occurring pairs. action: export — GraphML or JSON export for Gephi, yEd, Cytoscape, or code. Returns metrics, links, paths, gaps, or serialized graph data. Does not modify notes.

folder.description: [analyse] Limit analysis to notes in this folder

min_backlinks.description: [analyse] Minimum backlinks for dead_ends or stale

days.description: [analyse] Days threshold for stale or emerging_hubs

entity.description: [cooccurrence_gaps] Entity name to find gaps for

format.description: [export] Output format: graphml for Gephi/yEd/Cytoscape, json for programmatic use

include_cooccurrence.description: [export] Include co-occurrence edges between entities (default true)

min_edge_weight.description: [export] Minimum edge weight threshold for learned weighted edges

center_entity.description: [export] Center the export on this entity; only include nodes within depth hops

max_nodes.description: [export] Maximum nodes when center_entity is omitted (default 500)

name: init_semantic
title: Initialize Semantic Search
description: Run once to build vector embeddings for hybrid search. Produces an embedding index over all notes and entities. Returns embedding count and build duration. Does not run automatically — must be invoked explicitly. After building, search (action=query and action=similar) automatically uses semantic matching alongside BM25.

force.description: Clear and rebuild all embeddings from scratch (default: false)

name: insights
title: Insights
description: Vault intelligence and temporal analysis. action: evolution — how a concept changed over time. staleness — find outdated notes. context — what was happening around a date. note_intelligence — deep analysis of one note: quality score, missing links, stale sections, audit. growth — vault trends. Returns timelines, stale lists, or scores. Does not modify notes. e.g. { action:"staleness", threshold_days:60 } { action:"note_intelligence", path:"projects/acme.md" }

entity.description: [evolution] Entity/concept name to trace over time

threshold_days.description: [staleness] Notes not modified in this many days (default 30)

name: link
title: Link
description: Wikilink management. action: suggest — suggestions for text. action: feedback — accept/reject. action: unlinked — unlinked mentions. action: validate — broken links. action: stubs — stub candidates. action: dashboard — stats. action: unsuppress/timeline/layer_timeseries/snapshot_diff — advanced analytics. Returns suggestions, reports, or stats. Does not apply wikilinks. e.g. { action:"suggest", text:"Claude is an AI by Anthropic" } { action:"feedback", entity:"Claude", accepted:true }

text.description: [suggest] Text to analyze for wikilink suggestions

typos_only.description: [validate] Only report broken links with a similar existing note

group_by_target.description: [validate] Aggregate dead links by target, ranked by frequency

name: memory
description: Entity-linked vault facts and session summaries. action: store — save a fact (auto-links entities). get — by key. search — FTS5. list — browse. forget — delete. supersede — idempotent tombstone by thread_id or key (kept for audit). summarize_session. Returns stored content. Does not operate on vault note bodies. e.g. { action:"store", key:"sarah.pref" }

thread_id.description: [store|supersede|unsupersede] Thread correlation id (short GUID minted by the engine threads registry). store: stamps the fact. supersede: tombstones every current fact carrying the id. unsupersede: reverses a thread-resolution tombstone (self-tombstoned rows only).

reason.description: [supersede] Audit reason recorded on each superseded row (e.g. "thread-resolved: shipped in PR #12")

value.description: [store] The fact/preference/observation to store (up to 2000 chars)

visibility.description: [store] Memory visibility. Defaults to private when agent_id is supplied, otherwise shared

agent_id.description: [store|get|search|list|forget|summarize_session|brief] Agent identifier for scoped memory/session context

name: note
description: Create, move, rename, or delete a vault note. action: create — new note with optional content and frontmatter. action: move — relocate note (rewires backlinks). action: rename — rename file (rewires wikilinks). action: delete — remove note (requires confirm:true). Returns mutation result with path and rewired backlinks. Does not edit note content.

action.description: Operation to perform: create | move | rename | delete

path.description: Vault-relative path of the note to act on (e.g., "projects/my-note.md")

frontmatter.description: [create] Frontmatter fields (JSON object). Set type, aliases, description for best results.

expectedHash.description: [create] CAS precondition for overwrite: content hash of the on-disk file as last read (from read action=raw). Write fails with code WRITE_CONFLICT if the file changed since. Only meaningful with overwrite:true.

template.description: [create] Vault-relative path to a template file. Variables {{date}} and {{title}} are substituted.

suggestOutgoingLinks.description: [create] Append suggested outgoing wikilinks based on content

maxSuggestions.description: [create] Maximum number of suggested wikilinks to append (1-10, default: 5)

destination.description: [move] New vault-relative path to move the note to (e.g., "archive/my-note.md")

confirm.description: [delete] Must be true to execute deletion (safety guard)

updateBacklinks.description: [move|rename] If true (default), updates all backlinks pointing to this note

commit.description: If true, commit changes to git

dry_run.description: Preview changes without writing to disk

name: policy
description: Vault workflows. action: list — show policies. validate — check YAML and note refs. preview — dry-run. execute — run with compensating rollback on failure. author — generate YAML from description. revise — modify policy. Returns results or YAML. Does not run arbitrary code. e.g. { action:"list" } { action:"execute", policy:"weekly-review" }

policy.description: [preview|execute|revise] Policy name or full YAML content

variables.description: [preview|execute] Variables to pass to the policy

commit.description: [execute] If true, commit successful changes in one git commit after execution

description.description: [author] Description of what the policy should do

name: read
description: Read vault note content. action=structure (default): heading outline, frontmatter, backlinks, word count — prefer over built-in Read for vault notes. action=section: text under one heading. action=sections: vault-wide heading search by regex. action=raw: byte-exact content + hash (CAS/drift checks). Returns note content + metadata. Does not search or mutate.

action.description: Operation: structure (read note outline + metadata) | section (read one section by heading) | sections (vault-wide heading search) | raw (byte-exact file content + content hash, for CAS writers and drift reconciliation)

include_content.description: [structure] Include full section text under each heading (default false)

max_content_chars.description: [structure] Max total chars of section content (default 20000)

folder.description: [sections] Limit to notes in this folder

name: refresh_index
title: Refresh Index
description: Use when the vault index seems stale or after bulk external edits. Produces a full rebuild of the vault index and FTS5 search database. Returns rebuild duration and note count. Does not restart the server — only rebuilds the in-memory index.

name: schema
title: Schema
description: Inspect and evolve vault schema. action: overview — field usage stats. conventions — naming and required fields. folders — folder tree with note counts per folder. rename_field / rename_tag — bulk rename. migrate — update field values. validate — check notes against schema. Returns stats, conventions, or errors. Does not read note body content. e.g. { action:"overview" } { action:"rename_tag", old_name:"wip", new_name:"in-progress", dry_run:true }

path.description: [validate] Note path to validate (omit for whole vault)

name: search
description: Read-only query of vault notes. action=query (default): keyword/concept/date search. action=similar: content-overlap neighbours of a given note path. Returns ranked results with frontmatter, links, section provenance, and confidence scores; cross-vault search may also include partial_failure and vault_errors. Does not perform mutations.

query.description: [query] Search query text. Required for action=query unless using date filters. For folder/tags/frontmatter enumeration, use find_notes.

modified_after.description: [query] Only notes modified after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

modified_before.description: [query] Only notes modified before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

prefix.description: [query] Enable prefix matching for entity search (autocomplete)

limit.description: [query|similar] Maximum number of results to return

detail_count.description: [query] Number of top results to return with full metadata (backlinks, outlinks, headings, frontmatter). Remaining results get lightweight summaries. Default: 5.

context_note.description: [query] Path of the note providing context. When set, results connected to this note via weighted edges get an RRF boost.

consumer.description: [query] Output format: "llm" applies sandwich ordering and strips scoring fields for context efficiency. "human" preserves score order and all scoring metadata for UI display.

path.description: [similar] Path to the source note (relative to vault root, e.g. "projects/alpha.md"). Required for action=similar.

diversity.description: [similar] Relevance vs diversity tradeoff (0=max diversity, 1=pure relevance, default: 0.7)

name: tasks
title: Tasks
description: Use to list, filter, or toggle tasks in the vault. Returns task items with path, status, and text. Does not create tasks — use vault_add_task for that. action: list (filter by status/folder/tag/path) | toggle (check/uncheck by path + text match)

action.description: Operation: list=query tasks (default), toggle=check/uncheck a task

path.description: [list] Scope to tasks from this note path. [toggle] Note containing the task (required for toggle)

has_due_date.description: [list] Only return tasks with due dates

folder.description: [list] Limit to notes in this folder

tag.description: [list] Filter to tasks with this tag

section.description: [toggle] Limit task search to this section

name: vault_add_task
description: Use when adding a new task checkbox to a note section. Produces a markdown task line inserted under the specified heading. Returns the written task text with line number. Does not toggle existing tasks — use vault_toggle_task to check or uncheck.

section.description: Section to add the task to

completed.description: Whether the task should start as completed

commit.description: If true, commit this change to git (creates undo point)

skipWikilinks.description: If true, skip auto-wikilink application (wikilinks are applied by default)

suggestOutgoingLinks.description: Append suggested outgoing wikilinks based on content (e.g., "→ [[AI]], [[Philosophy]]"). Off by default — set true for daily notes, journals, or capture-heavy contexts.

maxSuggestions.description: Maximum number of suggested wikilinks to append (1-10, default: 5)

preserveListNesting.description: Preserve indentation when inserting into nested lists. Default: true

dry_run.description: Preview changes without writing to disk

name: vault_update_frontmatter
description: Use when setting or changing metadata fields on a note. Produces a frontmatter merge that preserves existing fields and adds or overwrites specified keys. Returns updated frontmatter object. Does not overwrite unspecified fields. Set only_if_missing to true to backfill without clobbering.

only_if_missing.description: If true, only add fields that don't already exist in the frontmatter (skip existing keys)

commit.description: If true, commit this change to git (creates undo point)

dry_run.description: Preview changes without writing to disk
